description_long: This object is a classic biplane with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by a series of struts and wires. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers towards the tail, with an open cockpit situated near the front, likely accommodating one or two seats. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a single tailwheel, arranged in a taildragger configuration. The nose features a visible propeller, indicating a piston engine mounted at the front. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a standard tail assembly. The entire structure appears to be constructed from a combination of metal framework and fabric or metal skin, with a matte black finish.
description: A vintage biplane with dual stacked wings, open cockpit, front-mounted propeller, and taildragger landing gear, finished in matte black.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and central propeller.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with vertical and horizontal stabilizers and the structure of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the propeller, landing gear, and the open cockpit's position relative to the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit likely configured for one or two seats, with no visible canopy.
